verses.text: Asaph cries aloud to God in trouble, seeking Him all night, yet 'my soul refused to be comforted.' His grief is heavy. Even the praying saint may find no quick relief.
Asaph cries aloud to God in trouble, seeking Him all night, yet 'my soul refused to be comforted.' His grief is heavy. Even the praying saint may find no quick relief.
Asaph cries aloud to God in trouble, seeking Him all night, yet 'my soul refused to be comforted.' His grief is heavy. Even the praying saint may find no quick relief.
Kept awake and too troubled to speak, he recalls 'the days of old' and communes with his own heart, searching. Memory becomes prayer. In sleepless distress he turns his thoughts back to God's past.

Kept awake and too troubled to speak, he recalls 'the days of old' and communes with his own heart, searching. Memory becomes prayer. In sleepless distress he turns his thoughts back to God's past.

He asks the hard questions: 'Will the Lord cast off for ever?... is his mercy clean gone?... hath God forgotten to be gracious?' Doubt voices its fears. The honest soul lays even its darkest doubts before God.

He asks the hard questions: 'Will the Lord cast off for ever?... is his mercy clean gone?... hath God forgotten to be gracious?' Doubt voices its fears. The honest soul lays even its darkest doubts before God.

He checks himself: 'this is my infirmity,' resolving to remember the LORD's right hand and meditate on His works. He chooses to recall, not brood. Deliberately rehearsing God's deeds drives back despair.

'Thy way, O God, is in the sanctuary,' and by His arm He redeemed His people. He fixes on God's holiness and power. Remembering redemption restores his confidence.

'Thy way, O God, is in the sanctuary,' and by His arm He redeemed His people. He fixes on God's holiness and power. Remembering redemption restores his confidence.

The sea saw God and trembled; His path was through mighty waters, yet 'thou leddest thy people like a flock' by Moses and Aaron. The God of the storm is the gentle Shepherd. The same power that splits seas tenderly guides His flock home.
